Motion For Default Judgment Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Aug-30-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar for a request for Interlocutory judgment against Defendant Marc Wang, an individual and successor trustee for the 2003 Pushin Wang Revocable Trust and Defendant The 2003 Pushin Wang Revocable Trust.

Dept.505; Judge: Michelle Tong; Clerk: K. Valdes; Proceedings reported by "For the Record" only to be used in compliance with California Government Code Section 69957, commencing at 10:21:12 a.m. and concluding at 10:46:50 a.m. (D505).

Enn Luo, Esq. representing Plaintiff Shao Ping Wang, an individual and as Trustee of the Shao Ping Wang Revocable Living Trust, u/a dated August 16, 2016 via Zoom Video Conference.
Plaintiff Shao Ping Wang was placed under oath and testified via Zoom Video Conference.. Plaintiff is assisted by Mandarin Speaking Interpreter Aidong Yi; 301135.
Defendant Marc Wang, an individual and successor trustee for the 2003 Pushin Wang Revocable Trust and Defendant The 2003 Pushin Wang Revocable Trust not appearing.

Court and counsel discuss the ownership of the property.
The Court understands that Plaintiff is requesting an interlocutory judgment for partition.
The Court examines the witness. Witness testifies.

Good cause appearing, The Court finds that the Plaintiff has presented sufficient evidence to award judgment, The Court grants Plaintiff's request for interlocutory judgment.

The Court finds that Plaintiff is entitled to a partition and that plaintiff is co-owner of the property with address 670 3rd Avenue, San Francisco, CA 94118.

Complete language and description is to be indicated on the proposed judgment to be submitted for review and approval of the court.

Monetary request for judgment is denied by the Court at this time.

Proposed judgment is to be prepared by Counsel and submitted to the court for review and approval.
